scope_classification.label: local_experiment
scope_classification.rationale: The topic can be explored with small simulations, synthetic data, or lightweight local experiments on the Mac.
scope_classification.feasibility_note: Yes, this topic is highly feasible on your MacBook M4, as the 36GB RAM and MPS GPU are well-suited for standard continual learning benchmarks, especially when fine-tuning pre-trained models. Suitable public datasets include Split CIFAR-100 for a classic vision benchmark, Permuted MNIST for a simpler sequential task, and CORe50 for a more challenging object recognition scenario. Experimenting with a standard model like ResNet-18 on Split CIFAR-100 across 5 tasks could take approximately 4-8 hours

research_gaps.text: Traceability gap: agents may produce ideas without an auditable chain from source evidence to experiment decision.
Review gap: simulated reviewer feedback is often generated after the fact instead of controlling whether an idea proceeds.
Experiment timing gap: many research agents run experiments late, after committing to an idea, rather than using cheap probes during ideation.
Failure memory gap: rejected ideas and failed probes are often lost instead of shaping the next search iteration.
Venue-fit gap: agent outputs often lack explicit comparison to workshop and conference expectations.

idea_tree.title: Idea Provenance Tracker for Continual Learning Systems
idea_tree.pitch: A system that tracks and visualizes the complete lineage of ideas and decisions in continual learning research.
idea_tree.mechanism: Implement a blockchain-inspired ledger system that records every idea generation, modification, and selection with timestamps and attribution.
idea_tree.metric: Decision quality score based on idea lineage awareness

idea_tree.title: Dynamic Review Feedback Generator for Continual Learning
idea_tree.pitch: An AI system that generates contextually relevant reviewer feedback for continual learning research ideas.
idea_tree.mechanism: Use a transformer model trained on actual peer reviews to generate realistic, nuanced feedback that considers the specific challenges of continual learning.
idea_tree.metric: Human reviewer agreement score with AI-generated feedback

idea_tree.title: Adaptive Experiment Scheduling for Continual Learning Research
idea_tree.pitch: A system that optimizes when to run experiments based on resource availability and research priorities.
idea_tree.mechanism: Implement a reinforcement learning agent that learns optimal experiment scheduling by considering computational costs, expected value, and time constraints.

idea_tree.title: Failure Pattern Repository for Continual Learning
idea_tree.pitch: A database that captures, categorizes, and retrieves failed approaches to prevent repeated mistakes in continual learning research.
idea_tree.mechanism: Create a structured knowledge graph of failed experiments, their contexts, and reasons for failure, with semantic search capabilities.

idea_tree.title: Idea Evolution Visualizer for Continual Learning
idea_tree.pitch: An interactive visualization tool that shows how ideas evolve over time in continual learning research.
idea_tree.mechanism: Build a network graph that traces idea connections, modifications, and evolutionary paths with interactive exploration capabilities.
idea_tree.metric: Time to identify promising research directions

idea_tree.title: Multi-Perspective Review System for Continual Learning
idea_tree.pitch: A system that generates reviews from multiple expert perspectives to provide comprehensive feedback on continual learning ideas.
idea_tree.mechanism: Train specialized models for different subdomains of continual learning and have them provide perspective-specific reviews.
idea_tree.metric: Idea robustness score based on multi-perspective feedback

fatal_flaws: The core concept of tracking idea lineage is not novel, as it directly applies existing data provenance and version control paradigms without a clear technical innovation.
The proposal is extremely vague, failing to define a unique value proposition, target users, or a specific research problem that this system would solve.
Missing falsifiable hypothesis; the proposal is an engineering artifact without a testable claim of utility.
Missing baseline comparisons and evaluation protocol; the system's effectiveness cannot be measured.
Missing details on reproducibility (seeds) and computational requirements.
Prior reviews indicate 3 fatal flaws in theory/mechanism and 3 in reproducibility, confirming these critical omissions.
The contribution is ambiguous; it's unclear if the novelty lies in the knowledge graph schema, the visualization technique, or the system itself, which is more of an infrastructure project.
Capturing the 'lineage of ideas' is fraught with difficulty; the system risks creating a misleadingly objective record of a highly subjective and contextual research process.
No public dataset specified or provided
No code repository or implementation details provided
fixable_flaws: The proposal lacks a defined hypothesis, dataset, and baseline for evaluation.
The technical implementation, data model, and visualization methods are completely unspecified.
The authors could define a clear hypothesis about the tracker's utility (e.g., debugging efficiency).
They could propose a benchmark suite of continual learning tasks and define relevant baselines for tracking provenance.
The paper needs to focus on a specific, narrow sub-domain of continual learning to demonstrate the system's value, rather than making a broad claim for all of CL research.
The evaluation is currently just a plan. It needs to be fleshed out into a concrete experimental protocol, such as a user study with specific tasks and metrics to measure the system's effectiveness.
Missing information about fixed seeds for random processes
No computational requirements or environment specifications provided
